item.title: Ares Private Credit Fund Posts Steepest Monthly Loss on Record
item.description: A private credit fund managed by Ares Management Corp. posted its steepest monthly loss on record in February, providing further evidence of deteriorating performance in the $1.8 trillion private credit market.

item.title: LNG Buyers Hunt for Deals in US After Qatar Is Shut From Market
item.description: Asian and European buyers of liquefied natural gas are chasing the limited number of cargoes still available from US suppliers after the world’s largest LNG plant in Qatar was shut out of the market by the war in Iran.

item.title: Gold Steadies as Investors Weigh Outlook for US-Iran Peace Talks
item.description: Gold steadied after a two-day recovery, as traders weighed conflicting statements from the US and Iran about negotiations to end the war in the Middle East.

item.title: RBA’s Kent Says Iran War Is Complicating Outlook for Policy
item.description: The Middle East conflict and associated energy supply shock threaten to further fan inflation in Australia at a time when its economy is already grappling with capacity pressures, a senior Reserve Bank official said.

item.title: Asian Stocks to Rise with US-Iran Talks in Focus: Markets Wrap
item.description: Asian equities were set to drift higher Thursday after gains for US stocks and bonds, as traders weighed the viability of US-Iran ceasefire talks.

item.title: Oil Rises as US and Iran Differ on Possible Routes to End War
item.description: Oil climbed as the US and Iran offered conflicting comments on efforts to end the war that’s shuttered the Strait of Hormuz and stoked concerns of a global energy crisis.

item.title: US Credit Market Grew More Dysfunctional in March, NY Fed Says
item.description: A New York Fed index on Wednesday signaled that the US corporate bond market saw more dislocations in March, with the high-grade bond market more bruised than its high-yield counterpart.

item.title: South Africa Seen Holding Rates as Iran Risks Persist
item.description: South Africa’s central bank looks set to keep borrowing costs on hold to assess the inflationary impact of the US-Israeli war on Iran, which has sent oil prices surging and pummeled the rand.

item.title: Japan Posts Longest Streak of First-Day IPO Flops Since 2020
item.description: Japan’s run of struggling stock market debuts has stretched to the longest in six years, another sign of investor sentiment being shaken by the war in Iran.

item.title: Janus Henderson Boosts Loan, Scraps Junk Bond Tied to Buyout
item.description: Banks led by JPMorgan Chase & Co. boosted a junk loan to support the purchase of Janus Henderson Group Plc by Nelson Peltz’s Trian Fund Management and General Catalyst to $2.6 billion after scrapping a planned bond sale amid volatile markets.

item.title: Is SpaceX Worth $1.75 Trillion? Key Questions for Musk’s Big IPO
item.description: In the seven and a half years since Apple Inc.’s market capitalization broke through the $1 trillion mark, investors have become accustomed to seeing big tech companies command 13-digit valuations in public markets.

item.title: Cairn Looks to Hire Americans to Build India’s Shale Sector
item.description: Vedanta Ltd.’s Cairn Oil & Gas is looking to quadruple daily output to the equivalent of 1 million barrels over the better part of the next decade as India’s largest private sector oil producers basks in the war-drive surge in crude prices.

item.title: Fed’s Miran Says He Raised Rate Projection Due to Inflation Data
item.description: Federal Reserve Governor Stephen Miran said he moved up his projection for where interest rates should end the year by half a percentage point in response to disappointing inflation data since officials last issued forecasts in December.

item.title: The EU’s Biggest Test for Device Makers: Replaceable Batteries
item.description: European Union rules requiring electronics to include easily replaceable batteries could force consumer tech makers to rethink their designs and go beyond the repairability improvements they’ve made in recent years.

item.title: IMF Runs Scenarios on Which Nations May Need Aid Due to Iran War
item.description: The International Monetary Fund is running scenarios on countries to gauge which economies could be in need of fresh financing if the Iran war drags on, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Morgan Stanley Warns Dollar Rally Won’t Last Long Amid Iran War
item.description: The dollar will weaken as interest-rate differentials between the US and Europe shrink and the war in Iran curbs economic growth, according to Morgan Stanley.

item.title: US to Make It Harder to Label Non-Banks ‘Too-Big-To-Fail’
item.description: Top US financial officials unveiled a proposal for non-bank firms to face a higher bar for regulators to potentially tag them as too-big-to-fail.

item.title: Mexico Bets on Supercomputer to Combat Extreme Weather Events
item.description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum is working to develop climate models that will help prevent extreme weather events, part of a push to set up a public supercomputer that can use millions of data points to tackle national challenges.

item.title: Bank of France Trims Growth Forecast, Sees Faster Inflation
item.description: The Bank of France reduced its 2026 economic-growth forecast and lifted its inflation prediction as it includes the impact of surging energy prices caused by the Iran war.

item.title: Dolce & Gabbana Gears Up for Lender Talks as Debt Pressure Rises
item.description: Dolce & Gabbana is embarking on fresh talks with lenders after weak global demand for luxury goods put pressure on earnings and the terms governing its debt, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: AI Doomer’s New Warning: Market Is Wrong to Ditch Rate-Cut Bets
item.description: A little over a month after James van Geelen sent stocks into a tailspin with his dystopian account of a post-AI economic collapse, he’s wagering that another fear-fueled selloff — this time, in the bond market — has run too far.

item.title: BofA Starts Private Capital M&A Team to Focus on PE Exits
item.description: Buyout firms are sitting on a massive stable of unsold companies. They’re holding them longer and looking for creative ways to monetize them, as the old bootstrap-and-flip nature of the business evolves.

item.title: The Problem with Private Credit ‘Working as Designed’
item.description: Liquidity at the gates.

item.title: Wealthsimple Joins Prediction Markets Fray as Canada Opens Up
item.description: More financial firms are wading into prediction markets in Canada, working to get the green light from regulators to bring a popular betting product to traders.

item.title: Apollo’s Insurance Arm Vaults to Second-Biggest FHLB Borrower
item.description: Apollo Global Management Inc.’s insurance arm was the second-biggest borrower last year in the Federal Home Loan Bank system, a Depression-era program designed to shore up mortgage lending that has morphed into a go-to — and controversial — source of cheap financing for banks and other financial institutions.

item.title: Italy Said to Plan Cutting Growth Forecast to as Low as 0.5%
item.description: The Italian government is set to cut its economic growth forecast, reflecting a hit from the war in the Persian Gulf.

item.title: Electronic Arts’ New Dollar Debt Rallies After $15 Billion Sale
item.description: Investors are bidding up Electronic Arts Inc. dollar-denominated debt that priced on Tuesday to help fund the video game maker’s record buyout.

item.title: Meta, Google Found Liable in Social Media Addiction Case
item.description: Meta Platforms Inc. and Alphabet Inc.’s Google were found liable for a 20-year-old woman who alleged that her addiction to the companies’ social media platforms caused her to suffer a mental health crisis, a jury said.

item.title: Musk’s xAI ‘Doubling Down’ on AI Videos After OpenAI Nixes Sora
item.description: Elon Musk said his artificial intelligence company, xAI, plans to upgrade its AI video generator, seizing on an opening left by rival OpenAI’s decision this week to discontinue its Sora product.

item.title: The Once-Sober ETF Industry Finds a Home on a Vegas Casino Floor
item.description: At 5:30 a.m. at the Virgin Hotels Las Vegas, the ETF industry’s biggest annual conference was taking place on the casino floor.
